file_name: PACIRA PHARMACEUTICALS, INC. - A_R STRATEGIC LICENSING, DISTRIBUTION AND MARKETING AGREEMENT .txt

perturbation.original_text: "Affiliate" With respect to any Party to this Agreement shall mean any company, corporation, firm, individual or other entity which Controls, is Controlled by or is under common Control with such Party to this Agreement for only so long as such Control exists;
perturbation.changed_text: "Affiliate" With respect to any Party to this Agreement shall mean any company, corporation, firm, individual or other entity which Controls, is Controlled by or is under common Control with such Party to this Agreement regardless of how long such Control exists;
perturbation.explanation: The modification alters the definition of 'Affiliate' by removing the condition 'for only so long as such Control exists'. The original definition implies that affiliate status ceases when control ceases. By removing this condition, the modified definition implies that once an entity is an affiliate, it remains so indefinitely, which could contradict how the term 'Affiliate' is used elsewhere in the contract where time-sensitive obligations are specified.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Subject to Section 20.2, neither Party shall, nor shall it purport to, assign, license, transfer or change any of its rights or obligations under this Agreement without the prior written consent of the other, such consent not to be unreasonably withheld conditioned or delayed; provided, however, that except as provided in Section 20.4 either Party may assign its rights hereunder to an Affiliate or to any successor by merger, consolidation, sale of stock or other equity interests or the sale of substantially all of the assets of such Party without the consent of the other Party. For the avoidance of doubt, either Party may grant a security interest with respect to its rights under this Agreement in connection with a secured financing or similar transaction.
perturbation.justification: The original definition of 'Affiliate' limited the affiliate status to the period of control. The modified definition removes this time constraint, implying perpetual affiliate status. This directly contradicts Section 20.2, which allows assignment of rights to an Affiliate. If an entity remains an Affiliate even after control ceases, a party could assign rights to a former affiliate long after the control relationship ended, potentially circumventing the intended restrictions on assignment without consent. This creates a significant contradiction because the definition of affiliate now impacts the scope and applicability of the assignment clause in a way not originally intended.

perturbation.original_text: "Confidential Information" Means all confidential information, data and materials in whatever form disclosed by or on behalf of one Party or its Affiliates to the other Party or its Affiliates including, without limitation, the terms of this Agreement, data, formulae, unpublished patent disclosures, processes, protocols, marketing studies, sales information, specifications and know-how, (and, in the case of EKR's Confidential Information, EKR's marketing plans and EKR's sales forecasts), but excluding information which either Party can establish by written documentation: (i) at the time of disclosure, is in the public domain or is public knowledge; (ii) after disclosure, becomes part of the public domain by publication, except by breach of any obligation of confidentiality by a Party hereto or an Affiliate of such Party; (iii) was already in its possession at the time of its receipt and was not acquired directly or indirectly from the other Party or its Affiliates; or (iv) received from Third Parties who were lawfully entitled to disclose such information;
perturbation.changed_text: "Confidential Information" Means all information, data, and materials, regardless of form, disclosed by either Party or its Affiliates to the other Party or its Affiliates, including, but not limited to, the terms of this Agreement, data, formulae, unpublished patent disclosures, processes, protocols, marketing studies, sales information, specifications, and know-how. This includes EKR's marketing plans and sales forecasts. There are no exceptions to what constitutes Confidential Information under this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of 'Confidential Information' includes explicit exclusions such as information already in the public domain. The modified definition removes these exclusions and explicitly states 'There are no exceptions,' thus directly contradicting the original clauses that allowed for certain information to be excluded from the definition of Confidential Information. This creates confusion about what information each party is obligated to keep confidential.
perturbation.contradicted_text: PPI and EKR undertake to each other to keep confidential, and to procure that their respective Affiliates, employees, directors, officers, contractors, lawyers and accountants (including those of their Affiliates) keep confidential, Confidential Information disclosed to it by or belonging to the other Party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly states 'There are no exceptions to what constitutes Confidential Information,' which directly contradicts the contradicted text, which assumes that confidential information can be disclosed if certain exceptions are met. This creates a strong in-text contradiction because the changed definition eliminates exclusions that are implicitly supported by the contradicted text, where PPI and EKR are obligated to keep confidential only 'Confidential Information disclosed to it by or belonging to the other Party.' The elimination of exceptions means that there's no implicit clause that there can be disclosed information, leading to an obligation to keep ALL information confidential.

perturbation.original_text: EKR shall pay to PPI, any Minimum Royalties and Additional Royalties due within forty-five (45) days of the end of each Quarter as the case may be subject to reconciliation at the end of each Calendar Year as set forth in Section 6.9.
perturbation.changed_text: EKR shall pay to PPI, any Minimum Royalties and Additional Royalties due immediately at the end of each Quarter as the case may be without reconciliation at the end of each Calendar Year as set forth in Section 6.9.
perturbation.explanation: The original text specifies that Minimum and Additional Royalties are due within 45 days of the quarter's end, subject to year-end reconciliation. The modified text mandates immediate payment at the end of each quarter and removes the reconciliation process. This directly contradicts the intent of reconciliation, which allows for adjustments based on cumulative sales and other factors, potentially leading to disputes over payment accuracy and timing.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Within forty-five (45) days of the end of each Contract Year, there shall be a reconciliation between the sums paid under Section 6.6 and the Royalties payable under Section 6.4, and any payment due (or in the event of an overpayment by EKR to PPI) such amounts shall be paid by one Party to the other within thirty (30) days of the resolution of such reconciliation.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the payment terms from 'within forty-five (45) days of the end of each Quarter' to 'immediately at the end of each Quarter' and removes the reconciliation process. This directly conflicts with the contradicted text, which states 'Within forty-five (45) days of the end of each Contract Year, there shall be a reconciliation'. By mandating immediate payment and eliminating reconciliation, the modified text directly contradicts the process outlined in the contradicted text, creating a clear in-text contradiction regarding payment timing and reconciliation obligations.
